title: Onosma popoviana
taxonomy: Onosma popoviana is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a member of the genus Onosma, which contains around 150 species of plants. The Onosma genus is part of the tribe Onosmeae and subfamily Boraginoideae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Further classification of Onosma popoviana may require more specific information on its taxonomic relationships.

intro: Onosma popoviana, also known as Popov's Onosma, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce the severity of fever. The bioactive constituents of Onosma popoviana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Onosma popoviana is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
